summary: No-ship. R-1 is closed and the red/green records are internally consistent and reachable, but R-2 substitutes a 1,000-put stress case for the original 40-put repro, R-3's mutation transcript is incomplete, and R-4's required no-op proof and canonical acceptance contract remain unresolved.

findings.title: Missing verification: original 40-put repro
findings.body: The claimed unminimised repro is not the original forty-put choreography. The replacement uses 40 workers for 25 rounds, totaling 1,000 puts, whereas the original adversarial case performs exactly one put for each of 40 tasks. For this timing-sensitive race, failure at the amplified workload does not prove the original 40-put scenario fails at red and disappears at green.
findings.recommendation: Commit a script-owned repro that preserves the original 40 total puts while adding non-vacuous pass/race witnesses, then regenerate both verify-records and verification output. Keep the 1,000-put test only as separate stress coverage.

findings.title: Missing verification: six R-3 gate mutations
findings.body: The committed mutation audit ends immediately after starting M-PRED-N0. It contains complete output only for IGN and FAIL; N0 has no result, and RC, DISC, LIST-RC, SIGPIPE, and OLDPARSER have no raw execution records. The stated 8/8 result therefore is not supported by the committed evidence required to prove that no gate predicate survived.
findings.recommendation: Commit uninterrupted raw output and exit status for all eight final-tree mutants, plus a final tally demonstrating zero survivors.

findings.title: Missing verification: decisive Phase G no-op mutant
findings.body: The transcript says M2 and M4 prove the new witness is non-vacuous, but M2 merely stops the test's external K-grave deletions while leaving production's remove branch active. After M3, the evidence block ends; no M-REMOVE-NOOP/M4 command or old-green/new-red output is committed. Thus the asserted decisive proof that the witness fails when production recovery does nothing is absent.
findings.recommendation: Commit the raw M-REMOVE-NOOP mutation transcript showing the old witness exiting 0 and the final witness failing, including exact commands, tree identities, output, and exit codes.

findings.title: Phase G acceptance contract still specifies the rejected vacuous witness
findings.body: The canonical plan still requires deleting every remaining grave and deriving remove-branch coverage from stepped_K—the exact construction R-4 found vacuous. The implementation now uses K_KILL/K_KEEP and garbage graves, but the release requirements still authorize the weak witness, leaving the plan and shipped test inconsistent.
findings.recommendation: Replace the stale Phase G row with the implemented K_KILL/K_KEEP contract, unlink-race witness, untouched keep class, garbage-grave corruption check, and required no-op mutation proof.

next_steps: Restore an exact 40-put script-owned repro and refresh red/green records.
Commit complete raw R-3 output for every declared mutation.
Commit the missing R-4 no-op mutation proof and update the Phase G plan contract.
Repeat the scoped release gate after the evidence artifacts are complete.
